items.note: The first Lake Lucerne round closed with a High Level Committee, three named working tracks and a 60-day roadmap toward a final deal — machinery, not compliance, because the public text creates organs and a deadline but not an enforceable sequence; Iran has already widened the relief claims beyond the mediator communiqué, and the Lebanon mechanism is the first live test.

The chokepoint moved from freeze to test passage: Kpler tracked Qatar-controlled LNG carriers and VLCCs transiting again and CENTCOM counted 55 merchant ships on Saturday, while Sunday traffic fell to a handful; Brent faded 2.09% to $78.89 on the Lucerne readout, but war-risk premia, the PGSA permit regime and an unconfirmed fee still sit over the route.

El Obeid faces ten consecutive days of RSF drone strikes and encirclement without verified ground entry; the Security Council has demanded the RSF halt an imminent assault on a city the Human Rights Council says puts roughly 500,000 civilians at risk, with the El Fasher playbook of reinforcement and aerial pressure running again before the ground push.

The Air Force pushed its Collaborative Combat Aircraft program into production and split the buy — two airframe awards (GA's FQ-42, Anduril's FQ-44) on one side, a six-vendor mission-autonomy pool on the other; airpower is becoming a platform market where the durable contest shifts toward reference architectures and replaceable behavior software.

items.what: An explosion and fire at Ras Laffan's Barzan facility left 54 injured and 18 missing; QatarEnergy called it an operational incident at a domestic-gas supply project — pipeline gas for local industry, power and desalination — not one of the 14 LNG export trains, keeping the casualty story and the export-arithmetic story separate without evidence of sabotage.

The post-October ceasefire toll passed 1,010 Palestinians killed; Israel's claim that the killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah had Hamas ties remains without public evidence — one named casualty inside a toll the lull has not stopped.

FERC ordered US grid operators to reform interconnection rules for data centers, treating speed-to-power as an AI-race variable — moving the frontier-compute contest from chips toward the electricity that runs them.

Russia said it downed roughly 301 Ukrainian drones overnight, and a Russian drone killed an Egyptian crew member on a merchant ship near Odesa — the long-range strike campaign now reaching neutral shipping in the export corridor.
Brent fell about 2% to $78.89 as the Strait resumed and the Lake Lucerne readout landed, the screen pricing returning barrels rather than a verdict that the route's risk has cleared.
